UNIT 08: 일치 (AGREEMENT)

주어와 동사의 수일치, 그 중에서도 수식어구, 도치, 부분 표현 등 복잡한 구조 속에서 정확한 주어를 찾아내는 능력을 마스터합니다.

Testing Point 22: 수식어구와 진짜 주어 완벽 구분법

전체 기출 빈도: ★★★★★ 최근 기출 빈도: ★★★★★

핵심 개념: 진짜 주어를 찾아라!
주어와 동사 사이에 삽입된 수식어구(전치사구, 분사구, 관계사절 등)에 현혹되지 않고, 문장의 핵심이 되는 '진짜 주어'를 찾아 동사의 수를 일치시켜야 합니다.

주요 수식어구 패턴

수식어구 유형 예시 (주어는 굵게 표시)
전치사구 The box [of apples] is heavy.
분사구 The man [talking to John] is my boss.
관계사절 The student [who wins the prize] receives a scholarship.
삽입어구 The CEO, [along with his team,] is visiting the factory.

⚠️ 함정 패턴: 복수 명사로 끝나는 전치사구

동사 바로 앞에 복수 명사가 위치하더라도, 그것이 전치사구의 일부라면 주어가 아닙니다.
The key [to the problems] lies... (주어는 key)

예제 분석: The only difference between grapes and raisins is / are that grapes have about 6 times as much water in them.

1. 수식어구 식별: `between grapes and raisins`는 주어를 수식하는 전치사구.
2. 진짜 주어 찾기: 수식어구를 제외한 진짜 주어는 `The only difference` (단수).
3. 동사 선택: 단수 주어에 맞춰 is가 정답.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (1/2)

  1. The cost of living in major cities is / are rising rapidly.
  2. The results of the experiment, which were published last month, supports / support our hypothesis.
  3. One of the main reasons for his success was / were his unwavering determination.
  4. The new regulations regarding environmental protection has / have been implemented.
  5. The professor, as well as her students, is / are excited about the upcoming conference.
  6. The development of new technologies is / are changing the way we live and work.
  7. The package sent by my parents contains / contain some of my favorite snacks.
  8. The details of the plan needs / need to be discussed further in the next meeting.
  9. The ability to communicate effectively is / are crucial for success in any field.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (2/2)

  1. The houses on that street, most of which were built in the 19th century, looks / look beautiful.
  2. The information provided in these documents seems / seem to be inaccurate.
  3. The team of researchers from different countries is / are collaborating on this project.
  4. The main character in these novels always solves / solve the mystery in the end.
  5. The fear of making mistakes often prevents / prevent people from trying new things.
  6. The effects of climate change on a global scale is / are becoming more and more evident.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

Testing Point 23: 주어가 동사 뒤로 갈 때 수일치 (도치)

전체 기출 빈도: ★★☆☆☆ 최근 기출 빈도: ★★☆☆☆

핵심 개념: 동사 뒤에 숨은 주어를 찾아라!
부정어구, 장소/방향 부사구, `There` 등이 문장 맨 앞에 올 경우 주어와 동사의 위치가 바뀝니다(도치). 이 경우 동사 뒤에 오는 명사가 진짜 주어이므로, 그 주어에 동사의 수를 일치시켜야 합니다.

주요 도치 유발 표현

도치 유발 표현 구조 예시 (주어는 굵게 표시)
장소/방향 부사구 부사구 + V + S Under the tree lies a dog.
부정어구 (Never, Not only 등) 부정어구 + 조동사/be + S + V Never have I seen such a thing.
There / Here There/Here + V + S There are many reasons.

* 주의: 주어가 대명사일 경우 장소/방향 부사구가 문두에 와도 도치되지 않습니다. (e.g., Here you are.)

예제 분석: In the back seat of the car next to mine was / were two sweet little boys.

1. 도치 식별: 문장 맨 앞에 장소 부사구(`In the back seat...`)가 왔으므로 도치 발생.
2. 진짜 주어 찾기: 동사 뒤에 있는 `two sweet little boys`가 진짜 주어 (복수).
3. 동사 선택: 복수 주어에 맞춰 were가 정답.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (1/2)

  1. On the wall hangs / hang several paintings by famous artists.
  2. Never before in our history has / have our country faced such a challenge.
  3. Under no circumstances should / are you give your password to anyone.
  4. There seems / seem to be a misunderstanding between the two parties.
  5. So beautiful was / were the scenery that we decided to stay for another day.
  6. Not only does / do this new technology save time, but it also reduces costs.
  7. Out of the darkness emerged / have emerged a strange figure.
  8. Little did / was he know that he was being watched.
  9. I love traveling, and so does / do my sister.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (2/2)

  1. He doesn't like spicy food, and neither am / do I.
  2. Among the trees stands / stand an old, abandoned house.
  3. Rarely has / have a political leader been so widely admired.
  4. Here comes / come the results of the final exam.
  5. Only after the sun sets does / do the nocturnal animals appear.
  6. At the heart of the issue is / are fundamental disagreements on policy.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

Testing Point 24: 부분 표현의 수일치 마스터

전체 기출 빈도: ★★☆☆☆ 최근 기출 빈도: ★★☆☆☆

핵심 개념: `of` 뒤가 왕이다!
`some of`, `most of`, `half of`, 분수, 퍼센트 등 부분을 나타내는 표현이 주어일 경우, 동사의 수는 `of` 뒤에 오는 명사의 수에 일치시킵니다.

주요 부분/분수 표현

`some of`, `most of`, `all of`, `the rest of`, `half of`, `a portion of`, `the majority of`, `one-third of`, `percent of` 등

표현 동사 수일치
some of the book (단수) → is
some of the books (복수) → are

⚠️ 절대 예외: `One of` vs `A number of`

One of the [복수명사] + 단수동사 (e.g., One of my friends is...)

A number of [복수명사] + 복수동사 (e.g., A number of friends are...)

The number of [복수명사] + 단수동사 (e.g., The number of friends is...)

예제 분석: Although the first five episodes of the drama had been filmed in black and white, the rest of the show was / were filmed and broadcast in color.

1. 주어 형태 확인: `the rest of the show`는 부분 표현.
2. `of` 뒤 명사 확인: `the show`는 단수 명사.
3. 동사 선택: 단수 명사에 맞춰 단수 동사 was가 정답.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (1/2)

  1. Most of the information provided in the report is / are outdated.
  2. Half of the employees has / have decided to join the new project.
  3. The rest of the students is / are waiting in the auditorium.
  4. Two-thirds of the planet's surface is / are covered by water.
  5. All of the equipment in the lab needs / need to be updated.
  6. One of the most important factors in learning a language is / are consistency.
  7. The number of tourists visiting this city has / have increased by 20% this year.
  8. A number of new policies is / are expected to be announced next week.
  9. Some of the advice he gave me was / were very helpful.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (2/2)

  1. The majority of the committee believes / believe that the proposal should be accepted.
  2. About 40 percent of the population lives / live below the poverty line.
  3. All of the cake has / have been eaten.
  4. The remainder of the funds is / are to be donated to charity.
  5. A portion of the profits goes / go directly to the investors.
  6. None of the students has / have completed the assignment yet. (Note: None can be singular or plural)
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

Testing Point 25: each/every는 단수, both는 복수

전체 기출 빈도: ★★☆☆☆ 최근 기출 빈도: ★☆☆☆☆

핵심 개념: 절대 규칙 암기
특정 수량형용사는 뒤따르는 명사의 수와 상관없이 항상 정해진 규칙에 따라 동사의 수를 결정합니다.

용법별 완전 분석

구분 표현 동사 예시
단수 취급 Each + 단수명사 단수 Each student has a book.
Each of the + 복수명사 단수 Each of the students has a book.
Every + 단수명사 단수 Every student has a book.
복수 취급 Both (of the) + 복수명사 복수 Both students are smart.
The + 형용사 복수 The rich often have problems.

예제 분석: In Colonial America, families were usually large, and every boy and girl was / were put to work at an early age.

1. 주어 형태 확인: `every boy and girl`.
2. 규칙 적용: `every`가 이끄는 주어는 `and`로 연결되더라도 항상 단수 취급합니다.
3. 동사 선택: 단수 주어에 맞춰 was가 정답.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (1/2)

  1. Each of the candidates has / have to give a presentation.
  2. Both of the answers you gave is / are correct.
  3. Every car and motorcycle on this road needs / need to be inspected.
  4. The unemployed in this city is / are receiving support from the government.
  5. Both the manager and the team leader agrees / agree with the new plan.
  6. Every piece of information is / are carefully checked for accuracy.
  7. The homeless requires / require more than just temporary shelters.
  8. Each employee receives / receive an annual performance review.
  9. Both of his parents is / are teachers at the local high school.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (2/2)

  1. Every attempt to solve the problem so far has / have failed.
  2. Each of the chapters in this book focuses / focus on a different topic.
  3. Every man, woman, and child was / were given a survival kit.
  4. The injured was / were quickly transported to the nearest hospital.
  5. Both of these reports contains / contain valuable data.
  6. Each question on the exam is / are worth ten points.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

Testing Point 26: 구와 절 주어는 단수 취급

전체 기출 빈도: ★★★★☆ 최근 기출 빈도: ★★★☆☆

핵심 개념: 하나의 덩어리(a single concept)로 간주하라!
동명사구(v-ing ~), to부정사구(to-v ~), 명사절(that/what/whether/의문사 ~)이 문장의 주어로 올 경우, 이를 하나의 개념 덩어리로 보아 항상 단수 동사로 받습니다.

주어 형태별 분석

주어 형태 동사 예시
동명사구 단수 Making new friends is sometimes difficult.
to부정사구 단수 To master a new skill requires time.
명사절 단수 What he said was true.

* 예외: 두 개의 동명사구나 to부정사구가 `and`로 연결되어 별개의 활동을 나타낼 때는 복수 취급합니다. (e.g., Reading books and watching movies are my hobbies.)

예제 분석: Throwing unneeded items out only hurts / hurt for a little while.

1. 주어 형태 확인: `Throwing unneeded items out`은 동명사구.
2. 규칙 적용: 동명사구 주어는 하나의 행위를 나타내므로 단수 취급.
3. 동사 선택: 단수 주어에 맞춰 3인칭 단수 현재형 동사 hurts가 정답.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (1/2)

  1. To understand different cultures is / are the first step toward global peace.
  2. What the researchers discovered challenges / challenge the existing theories.
  3. That the earth is round is / are a well-known fact.
  4. Learning a new language and understanding its culture goes / go hand in hand.
  5. How he managed to escape from the prison remains / remain a mystery to this day.
  6. Trying to solve all problems at once is / are not an effective strategy.
  7. Whether the new policy will be successful depends / depend on public cooperation.
  8. What seems easy at first often turns / turn out to be quite complicated.
  9. To be or not to be, that is / are the question.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (2/2)

  1. Collecting stamps and coins was / were his favorite pastime as a child.
  2. Why the dinosaurs disappeared so suddenly is / are still debated among scientists.
  3. To err is human; to forgive is / are divine.
  4. That you completed the marathon is / are truly amazing.
  5. Whatever you decide is / are fine with me.
  6. Washing your hands regularly helps / help prevent the spread of germs.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편
1

연습 문제 (1/2)

The cost of living in major cities is / are rising rapidly.
  1. ① is / are
2

연습 문제 (1/2)

The results of the experiment, which were published last month, supports / support our hypothesis.
  1. ① supports / support
3

연습 문제 (1/2)

One of the main reasons for his success was / were his unwavering determination.
  1. ① was / were

The new regulations regarding environmental protection has / have been implemented.
  1. ① has / have
5

연습 문제 (1/2)

The professor, as well as her students, is / are excited about the upcoming conference.
  1. ① is / are
6

연습 문제 (1/2)

The development of new technologies is / are changing the way we live and work.
  1. ① is / are

The package sent by my parents contains / contain some of my favorite snacks.

The details of the plan needs / need to be discussed further in the next meeting.
  1. ① needs / need
9

연습 문제 (1/2)

The ability to communicate effectively is / are crucial for success in any field.
  1. ① is / are

The houses on that street, most of which were built in the 19th century, looks / look beautiful.

The information provided in these documents seems / seem to be inaccurate.
  1. ① seems / seem

The team of researchers from different countries is / are collaborating on this project.
  1. ① is / are

The main character in these novels always solves / solve the mystery in the end.

  1. ① prevents / prevent

UNIT 09: 태 (VOICE)

동사의 태(능동태/수동태)를 결정하는 핵심 원리를 파악하고, 복잡한 시제와 결합된 수동태, 그리고 특별한 용법을 완벽히 마스터합니다.

Testing Point 27: 능동태 vs. 수동태 ('하느냐' vs. '되느냐')

전체 기출 빈도: ★★★★★ 최근 기출 빈도: ★★★★☆

핵심 개념: 주어와 동사의 의미 관계 및 목적어 유무
주어가 동작을 '하면' 능동태, '당하면' 수동태(`be p.p.`)를 씁니다. 또한, 타동사의 경우 능동태 뒤에는 목적어가 오지만, 수동태 뒤에는 목적어가 오지 않는 것이 일반적입니다.

⚠️ 절대 규칙: 자동사는 수동태 불가!

목적어를 갖지 않는 자동사는 수동태로 만들 수 없습니다.
주요 자동사: `happen`, `occur`, `arise`, `result`, `remain`, `consist of`, `disappear`, `emerge` 등
❌ The accident was happened. → ✅ The accident happened.

예제 분석: Excluding guide dogs, companion animals prohibited / are prohibited in the museums or the National Zoo.

1. 주어-동사 관계 파악: 주어 `companion animals`(반려동물)은 스스로 금지하는 것이 아니라, (규정에 의해) '금지되는' 대상. → 수동 관계.
2. 목적어 유무 확인: 동사 뒤에 목적어가 없음.
3. 동사 선택: 수동태인 are prohibited가 정답.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (1/2)

  1. The new policy will implement / be implemented from next month.
  2. A serious problem has arisen / been arisen in the production line.
  3. The results of the study published / were published in a famous scientific journal.
  4. All applicants are required to submit / be submitted their forms by the deadline.
  5. The concert was cancelled because not enough tickets had sold / been sold.
  6. The building, which designed / was designed by a world-renowned architect, won an award.
  7. The package I sent last week has not arrived / been arrived yet.
  8. This issue needs to discuss / be discussed in the next meeting.
  9. The letter is writing / is being written by the secretary right now.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (2/2)

  1. The ancient ruins discovered / were discovered by a team of archaeologists.
  2. The new software is expected / is expecting to increase productivity significantly.
  3. The changes that have occurred / been occurred in our society are irreversible.
  4. He was offered a scholarship, which he gladly accepted / was accepted.
  5. The rules must follow / be followed by everyone without exception.
  6. The mystery of the Bermuda Triangle has never fully explained / been explained.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

Testing Point 28: be used to-v vs. be used to v-ing

전체 기출 빈도: ★★☆☆☆ 최근 기출 빈도: ★★☆☆☆

핵심 개념: '목적'과 '익숙함'의 의미 차이 구분
`used`와 관련된 세 가지 표현은 형태가 비슷하지만 의미가 완전히 다르므로, 주어와 문맥을 통해 정확히 구분해야 합니다.

'used'의 세 가지 얼굴

표현 주어 의미
used to + 동사원형 사람/사물 ~하곤 했다 (과거의 습관/상태)
be used to + 동사원형 사물 ~하는 데 사용되다 (수동태, 목적)
be used to + v-ing/명사 사람 ~하는 데 익숙하다 (상태, 습관)

예제 분석: The Masai don't slaughter their cattle for food; but if a cow is killed, then the hides are used to make / making shoes, clothing, and bed coverings.

1. 주어 확인: `the hides` (가죽) → 사물.
2. 의미 파악: 가죽이 신발을 '만드는 데 사용되다'는 목적/용도를 나타냄.
3. 구문 선택: `be used to + 동사원형` 구문.
4. 결론: 동사원형 make가 정답.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (1/2)

  1. This special software is used to analyze / analyzing large amounts of data.
  2. After living in Canada for ten years, she is used to speak / speaking English every day.
  3. I used to / am used to play basketball a lot when I was in high school.
  4. He isn't used to work / working under such high pressure.
  5. Wood can be used to build / building houses and furniture.
  6. It took me a while to get used to drive / driving on the left side of the road.
  7. This tool used to / is used to be very popular, but now it's obsolete.
  8. Are you used to eat / eating spicy food?
  9. The internet is widely used to access / accessing information from all over the world.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (2/2)

  1. There used to / was used to be a big tree in front of my house.
  2. As a doctor, he is used to see / seeing difficult situations.
  3. Computers are used to process / processing vast quantities of information.
  4. She used to / is used to have long hair, but she cut it short last year.
  5. I will never get used to live / living in such a noisy city.
  6. This key is used to open / opening the safe deposit box.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편
1

연습 문제 (1/2)

The new policy will implement / be implemented from next month.
  1. ① implement / be implemented
2

연습 문제 (1/2)

  1. ① arisen / been arisen
3

The results of the study published / were published in a famous scientific journal.
  1. ① published / were published

  1. ① discuss / be discussed
9

  1. ① is writing / is being written

UNIT 10: 시제, 법과 조동사

단순과거와 완료시제의 정확한 구분, 가정법의 핵심 원리, 그리고 조동사의 다양한 의미와 시제 표현을 완벽히 마스터합니다.

Testing Point 29 & 30: 완료시제의 이해

핵심 개념: 기준 시점 파악
- 단순과거 vs. 현재완료: 명백한 과거 시점 부사(구)의 유무가 결정적 단서.
- 현재완료 vs. 과거완료: 문장의 기준 시점이 '현재'인가 '과거'인가가 핵심.

완료시제 매칭 가이드

시제 시간 표현 / 개념 예시
단순과거 `yesterday`, `... ago`, `in 2010` I visited Paris `last year`.
현재완료 `since`, `for`, `so far` (과거 → 현재) I have lived here `for ten years`.
과거완료 대과거 (과거 → 더 이전 과거) He realized he had lost his wallet.

예제 분석 1 (TP29): In the summer of 2005, he visited / has visited China...

`In the summer of 2005`는 명백한 과거 시점이므로 단순과거 visited가 정답.

예제 분석 2 (TP30): The police realized that they have made / had made a terrible mistake.

기준 시점 `realized`(과거)보다 실수를 한 것이 더 이전이므로 대과거를 나타내는 과거완료 had made가 정답.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (1/2)

  1. She graduated / has graduated from university three years ago.
  2. We lived / have lived in this city since 2015.
  3. The company was founded / has been founded in 1990 by two brothers.
  4. I saw / have seen that movie last weekend with my friends.
  5. Technology changed / has changed dramatically over the last few decades.
  6. When did you finish / have you finished your project?
  7. He worked / has worked as a teacher for 20 years before he retired.
  8. So far, the team won / has won all of its matches this season.
  9. Shakespeare wrote / has written many famous plays and sonnets.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (2/2)

  1. I didn't receive / haven't received any news from him lately.
  2. When I arrived at the party, most of the guests already left / had already left.
  3. She told me she has never seen / had never seen snow before she moved to Canada.
  4. The movie already started / had already started by the time we got to the cinema.
  5. He lived / had lived in London for five years before he moved to Paris last year.
  6. I couldn't get into my apartment because I forgot / had forgotten my keys.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

Testing Point 31: 시간/조건 부사절의 시제

핵심 개념: 현재가 미래를 대신한다!
시간(`when`, `as soon as` 등)이나 조건(`if`, `unless` 등)을 나타내는 부사절에서는 미래의 내용일지라도 미래시제 `will`을 쓰지 않고 현재시제를 사용합니다.

⚠️ 주의사항: 명사절에서는 미래시제 사용!

동일한 접속사라도 명사절(주어, 목적어, 보어 역할)을 이끌 때는 미래의 일을 미래시제로 표현합니다.
I don't know if he will come tomorrow. (`if`절이 `know`의 목적어)

Testing Point 32: 가정법 과거 vs. 과거완료

핵심 개념: 시제 불일치 원리
가정법은 실제 사실과 반대되는 상황을 가정하므로, 시제를 한 단계씩 과거로 돌려 표현합니다.

가정법 공식 완전 정리

구분 If절 (조건절) 주절
가정법 과거
(현재 사실 반대)		 If + S + 과거동사/were... S + would/could + 동사원형...
가정법 과거완료
(과거 사실 반대)		 If + S + had p.p.... S + would/could + have p.p....
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (1/2)

  1. As soon as she finishes / will finish her work, she will join us for dinner.
  2. Please let me know if you need / will need any help with your assignment.
  3. We won't start the meeting until everyone arrives / will arrive.
  4. I wonder when the new store opens / will open next month.
  5. Unless the weather improves / will improve, we will have to postpone the match.
  6. If I knew / had known you were coming, I would have baked a cake.
  7. If he were here now, he would know / would have known what to do.
  8. We could have won the game if our best player wasn't / hadn't been injured.
  9. If I had more time, I will / would travel around the world.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (2/2)

  1. If the rescue team had arrived sooner, more lives could be / could have been saved.
  2. By the time you read / will read this email, I will be on a plane.
  3. I am not sure if it rains / will rain tomorrow.
  4. If I were you, I would accept / would have accepted the offer immediately.
  5. She would have called you if she knew / had known your number.
  6. What would you do if you win / won the lottery?
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

Testing Point 33: 당위성 that절의 (should+)동사원형

핵심 개념: 당위성(~해야 한다)이 있으면 (should+)동사원형!
'주장, 제안, 요구, 명령' 동사(`suggest`, `insist` 등)나 '중요, 필수' 형용사(`important`, `essential` 등) 뒤의 that절 내용이 '~해야 한다'는 당위성을 가질 때, 동사는 `(should) + 동사원형`을 씁니다.

⚠️ 주의사항: '사실'을 주장할 때는 직설법!

같은 동사라도 that절이 '사실'을 묘사하거나 주장할 때는 시제 일치 규칙을 따릅니다.
The witness insisted that the accident happened... (사고가 일어났다고 '사실을' 주장)

Testing Point 34: 조동사 + have p.p.

핵심 개념: 과거에 대한 이야기
`조동사 + have p.p.` 형태는 현재나 미래가 아닌, 과거 사실에 대한 추측, 후회, 유감을 나타냅니다.

주요 표현

표현 의미
must have p.p. ~했음에 틀림없다 (강한 긍정 추측)
cannot have p.p. ~했을 리가 없다 (강한 부정 추측)
may/might have p.p. ~했을지도 모른다 (약한 추측)
should have p.p. ~했어야 했는데 (하지 않아서 후회/유감)
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (1/2)

  1. The committee recommended that the new rule is / be implemented immediately.
  2. Many scientists insist that climate change is / be a serious threat to humanity.
  3. The law requires that all drivers have / has car insurance.
  4. The manager demanded that the report was / be finished by Friday.
  5. Her expression suggested that she knew / know the secret already.
  6. The lights are off in his room. He must go / must have gone to bed.
  7. She looks very happy. She may pass / may have passed the exam.
  8. He didn't answer my call. He could not hear / could not have heard the phone.
  9. It was a mistake to sell the house. We should not sell / should not have sold it.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

연습 문제 (2/2)

  1. I can't find my keys anywhere. I might leave / might have left them in the car.
  2. It is imperative that the government takes / take action on this issue.
  3. The empty plate suggests that he ate / eat all the food.
  4. You look exhausted. You should take / should have taken a break earlier.
  5. He arrived late for the crucial meeting. He must be / must have been stuck in traffic.
  6. It's strange that she isn't here. She might forget / might have forgotten about our appointment.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

🔥 단원 마무리 테스트 (1/2)

  1. The list of candidates for the position has / have been finalized.
  2. Not only is / does she a talented musician, but she also excels in sports.
  3. Most of the work was / were completed ahead of schedule.
  4. Each of the team members is / are responsible for a specific task.
  5. The new machine is used to produce / producing high-quality components.
  6. If I had / had had enough money at that time, I would have bought the car.
  7. The manager suggested that the meeting is / be postponed.
  8. He can't find his wallet. He must leave / must have left it at the restaurant.
  9. I will call you as soon as I arrive / will arrive at the airport.
  10. The problem that has arisen / been arisen needs immediate attention.
  11. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 찾으시오.
    The ⓐnumber of people attending the conference ⓑwere surprisingly high, which ⓒindicates a growing interest in the ⓓtopic.
  12. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 찾으시오.
    If the weather ⓐhad been better yesterday, we ⓑwould go for a hike in the mountains instead of ⓒstaying ⓓindoors.
  13. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 찾으시오.
    The witness insisted that the accident ⓐoccurred at the intersection, and demanded that a new traffic light ⓑwas installed.
  14. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 찾으시오.
    The documents ⓐdiscovered during the investigation ⓑis now ⓒbeing analyzed by the experts.
  15. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 찾으시오.
    He is not used to ⓐlive in a big city, as he ⓑused to ⓒlive in a small town his whole life.
Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

🔥 단원 마무리 테스트 (2/2)

  1. 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
    One of the most common mistakes made by learners are confusing similar-sounding words.
  2. 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
    Never before I have seen such a beautiful sunset.
  3. 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
    She regrets not to study harder for the exam she took last week.
  4. 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
    The package I have sent two days ago has not arrived yet.
  5. 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
    It is essential that every employee follows the safety regulations.
  6. 빈칸에 가장 적절한 것을 선택하시오.
    Half of the apples in the basket _______ rotten.
    ① is ② are ③ has ④ have
  7. 빈칸에 가장 적절한 것을 선택하시오.
    If he _______ more careful, the accident would not have happened.
    ① was ② were ③ had been ④ has been
  8. 빈칸에 가장 적절한 것을 선택하시오.
    There _______ a number of issues that need to be addressed.
    ① is ② are ③ was ④ were
  9. 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.

  1. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    Among the explanations for our nation's high divorce rate (A) is / are that we have such strong expectations of marriage. We expect our spouse to be a lover, a friend, a counselor, a career person, and a parent. For example, in one survey (B) conducted / conducting by Epstein in 1981, unhappily married people, especially men, expressed unrealistic expectations about marriage. On the other hand, those with happy marriages showed the most respect toward their spouses. In short, the most important thing that happily married couples have in common is not to expect too much but (C) respected / to respect each other.

  2. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    What does the phrase “healthy eating” mean to you? A decade ago, that phrase meant something completely different to me than it ① does now. I ② used to believe healthy eating meant consuming only a small amount of fat or calories for each meal. I had no idea how to read the list of ingredients on a food item, nor ③ did I understand the importance of looking at the Nutrition Facts label. I only concentrated on ④ what I shouldn't eat. However, my extreme dieting eventually had a detrimental impact on my health. I realized that focusing on nutritious whole foods that I should eat ⑤ to allow me to have a healthy, balanced diet without feeling deprived.

  3. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    Nicknames and their associated identities, in the emerging medium of online communication, ⓐ has become an important means of identifying the participants in chat rooms and newsgroups. H. Bays, a sociolinguist, observes that online, a nickname is anything from numbers and punctuation to a highly personal or expressive name and ⓑ containing numerous clues to gender, age, music and sports interests. Look at *snowwhite12*, *lion66*, *musiclover33*, and *Birdie_sunneyman*, for example. She further points out that the nickname is the first sign of individuality ⓒ when one encounters another participant. It serves as a first impression and shows the “face” ⓓ that its creator wants everyone else to “see” online.

  1. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 모두 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    I remember one of the smartest I.T. executives for whom I ever worked strongly ⓐ resisting the movement to measure programmer productivity that was popular at the time. He said that you can never tell whether they are working by looking at them. Picture two programmers ⓑ work side by side. One is leaning back in his chair with his eyes ⓒ closing and his feet on the desk. The other is working hard, ⓓ typing code into his computer. The one with his feet up could be thinking, and the other one may be too busy ⓔ typing to give it enough thought. In the end, the busy typist could well produce ten times as many lines of code as the thinker, which contain twice as many new problems as the thinker's.

  2. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    The ability to delay gratification is one of the most effective personal skills you can develop. Research shows that people with this skill (A) is / are more successful in their careers and personal lives. The famous marshmallow experiment, for instance, had children (B) to wait / wait in a room with a marshmallow. They were told they could eat it now, or wait 15 minutes and get two. Years later, the children who waited were found to be more competent. This suggests that teaching children patience and self-control (C) is / are crucial for their future success.

  3. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    When we are in a stressful situation, our brains release cortisol, a hormone that prepares us for a 'fight or flight' response. While this is useful for short-term crises, chronic stress keeps cortisol levels high, ① which can be harmful. High cortisol levels can suppress the immune system, making us more susceptible to infections. It also interferes with learning and memory, and ② contributes to weight gain. Therefore, it is essential that we ③ find effective ways to manage stress. Techniques such as mindfulness, exercise, and social support have been shown ④ to lower cortisol levels and improve overall well-being, ⑤ that is a key to a healthy life.

  1. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    One of the most powerful tools for learning ⓐ lie in our ability to make connections between new information and what we already know. This process, known as elaboration, helps to encode memories more deeply. For example, if you are trying to remember the concept of 'photosynthesis,' you might connect it to your existing knowledge of plants, sunlight, and energy. The more connections you make, the more retrieval paths you create, ⓑ making it easier to access the information later. This is why simply rereading notes is not as effective as ⓒ is actively trying to explain the concepts to someone else, ⓓ which forces you to elaborate and form connections.

  2. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    Despite ① being a common practice, multitasking is a myth. The human brain is not wired to focus on two demanding tasks at once. Instead, what we perceive as multitasking is actually rapid task-switching. Every time we switch, there is a cognitive cost, ② which our performance on both tasks suffers. This is why texting while driving is so dangerous; the brain cannot dedicate enough resources to either task, ③ led to slower reaction times. To be more productive, it's far better to focus on a single task for a dedicated period, a practice known as monotasking. This allows for deeper concentration and ④ ultimately produces higher quality work than task-switching ⑤ does.

  3. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    The rise of social media has fundamentally changed how we interact. While it offers unprecedented opportunities for connection, it also presents unique challenges. One major concern is the curated nature of online personas, where people present an idealized version of their lives. This can lead to social comparison and feelings of inadequacy among users. Moreover, the algorithms that power these platforms are designed to keep us (A) engaging / engaged, often by showing us content that is emotionally charged. It is important that we (B) are / be mindful of these dynamics. We should strive to use these tools in a way that (C) foster / fosters genuine connection rather than superficial comparison.

    The ancient library of Alexandria, ⓐ founding in the 3rd century BC, was one of the most significant libraries of the ancient world. It is believed to have housed hundreds of thousands of scrolls and ⓑ to have been a major center for scholarship. Scholars from all over the world were invited ⓒ study there. Unfortunately, the library was destroyed in a series of events over several centuries. The loss of the knowledge ⓓ contained within its walls is considered one of the greatest intellectual tragedies in history, and it reminds us how fragile cultural heritage can be.

  1. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    The majority of the plants we have in our house or office (A) is / are actually from the tropics. They thrive in humidity, and most houses and offices aren't very humid. Misting isn't a true solution, (B) although / despite what you may have heard. It's embarrassing for both you and the plant, and the water evaporates immediately anyway. A cool way to solve the humidity problem is to fill a little shallow dish with water and pebbles and (C) places / place it near your plant or under it. The water evaporates slowly off the rocks and provides a little more moisture in the air around your plant.

  2. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    A cat typically lands on his feet when he falls from a high place. His body reflexively corrects its course ① so that by the time he reaches the ground, his feet are positioned to hit first. A cat who is falling from great heights ② begins to shift his balance immediately after his fall begins. His body figures out which side should be up, and he begins ③ rotating his head until he's facing that way. Next his spine follows as he arches his back; then he positions his front feet and back legs under him, with his front paws placed near his face to protect ④ it from the ground's impact. Cats are also born with flexible backbones. While humans have just 24 vertebrae, they have 30, which ⑤ contribute to their flexibility.

  3. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    A centenarian is a person who is at least 100 years old. Centenarians are honored by television shows like NBC's *Today*, ⓐ where a person aged at least 100 is shown on the weather report each day. When this practice first started many years ago, there were few centenarians and everyone wanted ⓑ to honor like them. Recently, however, the number of centenarians has increased so dramatically ⓒ which those shown are randomly selected. The last census reported that there were over 35,000 centenarians ⓓ living in the United States, more than double the number ten years ago.

  1. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 모두 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    There is a fascinating history to the word “gossip,” which tells us ⓐ how isolated we have become in our communities. In medieval Ireland, ⓑ because the men spent the days in the fields and so many women died in childbirth or of disease, there were few adults to watch over the children. It became the custom for anyone in the community who saw or spoke to a child in the course of the day's work to pass on ⓒ that information immediately to anyone else in the community. That constant concern with the children ⓓ called “gossiping” the children, and it was meant to keep them under the concerned watch of the adult community. Now when we ask our children where they are going, “gossip” has become a negative word, ⓔ implied inappropriate interest in another's affairs.

  2. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    Sleep is not just a period of inactivity; it is a crucial process for memory consolidation. During deep sleep, the brain replays the day's events, strengthening the neural connections that form memories. This is why getting a good night's sleep after studying (A) is / are so effective. The information you learned is transferred from short-term to long-term storage. Furthermore, a lack of sleep can impair the brain's ability to form new memories. When you are sleep-deprived, the hippocampus, a key brain region for memory, becomes less active, (B) makes / making it harder to learn. This makes (C) that / it clear that sleep is as important to learning as the studying itself.

  3. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    The concept of 'placebo effect' is fascinating. It occurs when a patient experiences real improvements in their condition after ① receiving a treatment with no active therapeutic substance, like a sugar pill. This effect highlights the powerful connection between the mind and body. The patient's belief in the treatment can trigger physiological changes, ② such as the release of endorphins, which are natural painkillers. This is why new drugs are tested against placebos in clinical trials. It is essential that researchers ③ determine whether a drug's effects are due to its chemical properties or simply the patient's expectation. The placebo effect doesn't mean an illness is imaginary; rather, it shows that our beliefs ④ are a powerful component of healing, a factor that modern medicine is still trying to fully ⑤ be understood.

  1. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    In the world of artificial intelligence, there is a distinction between 'narrow AI,' which is designed for a specific task, and 'general AI,' ⓐ what could perform any intellectual task a human can. Most of ⓑ what we see today, from virtual assistants to self-driving cars, ⓒ are narrow AI. Creating general AI is a far more complex challenge. It would require an AI to possess common sense, creativity, and emotional intelligence—qualities that are difficult to program. Some experts believe that achieving general AI is inevitable, while ⓓ others think it may never be possible.

  2. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    The Great Wall of China is not a single, continuous wall but a series of fortifications. ① Built over centuries by different dynasties, its primary purpose was to protect Chinese states and empires against raids and invasions. While the wall is an incredible feat of engineering, the popular belief that it is visible from the moon with the naked eye ② is untrue. Not only ③ is it too narrow, but its color also blends in with the surrounding terrain. This myth likely arose from a desire to express the sheer scale and magnificence of the structure. The true wonder of the wall ④ lies not in its visibility from space, but in the immense human effort it represents, a testament to the perseverance of ⑤ that who built it.

  3. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    Confirmation bias is the tendency to search for, interpret, and recall information in a way that confirms one's preexisting beliefs. This cognitive bias is one of the most significant hurdles to objective thinking. For example, a person who believes a certain political party is corrupt (A) is / are more likely to notice and remember news stories that support that view, while ignoring stories that contradict it. This makes (B) it / that difficult to have a productive debate, as each side is operating from a different set of facts. To overcome this bias, it is recommended that one actively (C) seek / seeks out information from sources with opposing viewpoints.

    The discovery of penicillin by Alexander Fleming in 1928 is a classic example of serendipity in science. Fleming had left a petri dish containing bacteria ⓐ contaminating by a mold. He noticed that the bacteria around the mold ⓑ had been killed. This observation led him ⓒ to isolate the substance responsible: penicillin. However, it was Howard Florey and Ernst Chain who later developed a method to mass-produce the drug. Without their work, Fleming's discovery might have remained a scientific curiosity. This story shows that scientific breakthroughs often require not only a keen observation but also the dedication ⓓ to develop that observation into a practical application.

🔥 실전 모의고사 | 3회 (10문항)

  1. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    Tossing stuff out is difficult for many people. Professional organizers are prepared for this resistance and wield an array of techniques to break (A) it / them down. One trick is pushing the client into throwing as many items as possible into large, black trash bags that can be whisked away before second thoughts set in. Another is having the client (B) kept / keep a photograph of the object as a substitute for the item. One organizer says that when a client exhibits a strong emotional attachment to an item, she makes the client talk to the item as if it were a friend (C) embarrass / to embarrass the client into realizing how silly it is to be overly tied to an object.

  2. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    Human beings differ in many ways and have a variety of tastes; different things appeal more to one person than to ① another. You may never feel about Newton the way you feel about Shakespeare, either because you may be able to read Newton so well that you do not have to read him again, or because mathematical systems of the world just do not have all that ② much appeal to you. Or, if they ③ are — Charles Darwin is an example of such a person — then Newton may be one of the handful of books that ④ are great for you, and not Shakespeare. There is no particular book or group of books that must be great for you, but you should seek out the ⑤ few books that have value for you.

  3. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    One of the most significant advances during the Renaissance ⓐ were the development of a new attitude toward the acquisition of knowledge itself. The scientific method worked to understand the world through empirical observation and experiment. Considerable progress ⓑ was made in such fields as astronomy, physics, and anatomy, but science soon came into conflict with the Roman Catholic Church. When Copernicus, on the basis of his observations, suggested that the Sun, and not the Earth, ⓒ be the point about which the planets revolved, the Church banned his book. Galileo demonstrated that the planets are held in their orbits by physical laws, which led to ⓓ him spending the last eight years of his life under house arrest.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

🔥 실전 모의고사 | 3회

  1. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 모두 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    The neurotransmitter dopamine moves between neurons in different areas of the brain that ⓐ control the way in which people sleep, eat, and move. When people experience external stimuli, dopamine is released into neural pathways to the reward system. This tells people ⓑ that they're doing is pleasing, and they should continue to do it. For example, research shows that screens cause dopamine to be released into the pathways, which wears down these pathways and ⓒ increase the desire for greater stimuli. The rush of dopamine experienced by children watching screens ⓓ strains their brain's reward system before it has fully developed. Their brains crave more and more dopamine while producing less dopamine naturally.

  2. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    The Dunning-Kruger effect is a cognitive bias where people with low ability at a task overestimate their ability. It is related to the metacognitive inability of the unskilled to recognize their own ineptitude. This is (A) because / why they cannot objectively evaluate their own performance. Conversely, highly skilled individuals may underestimate their relative competence, erroneously assuming that tasks which are easy for them (B) is / are also easy for others. This phenomenon was first observed by David Dunning and Justin Kruger, (C) whose / which research has had a significant impact on psychology.

  3. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    The imposter syndrome is a psychological pattern in which an individual doubts their accomplishments and has a persistent internalized fear of ① being exposed as a "fraud." Despite external evidence of their competence, those experiencing this syndrome remain convinced that they are frauds and do not deserve all they have achieved. They often attribute their success to luck or to ② deceiving others into thinking they are more intelligent than they perceive themselves to be. It is important that anyone suffering from this ③ seeks professional help, as it can lead to anxiety and depression. Psychologists recommend that they ④ acknowledge their feelings and ⑤ share them with trusted friends or mentors.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

🔥 실전 모의고사 | 3회

  1. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    The concept of "chunking" in psychology refers to the process of taking individual pieces of information and grouping them into larger units. By grouping each piece into a large whole, you can improve the amount of information you can remember. For example, a phone number sequence of 4-7-1-1-3-2-4 is difficult to remember. However, if you chunk it into 471-1324, it becomes much easier. This is because our short-term memory is only capable of holding a limited number of items, typically around seven. Chunking allows us ⓐ overcoming this limitation by making each item ⓑ contain more information. This strategy ⓒ is used widely in everyday life, from memorizing credit card numbers ⓓ to learn new languages.

  2. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    The bystander effect is a social psychological phenomenon where individuals are less likely to offer help to a victim when other people are present. The greater the number of bystanders, the less likely it is that any one of them ① will help. Several factors contribute to this. One is diffusion of responsibility: when others are present, individuals feel less personal responsibility to act, ② assumed someone else will. Another is the need to behave in socially acceptable ways; if other people are not reacting, individuals often interpret the situation as a non-emergency. This makes ③ it crucial to single out an individual in a crowd when you need help. By pointing to someone and saying, "You, in the blue shirt, call 911," you eliminate the diffusion of responsibility and ④ make them ⑤ feel directly responsible.

  3. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    The ocean's "twilight zone," the dimly lit area between 200 and 1,000 meters deep, (A) teem / teems with life. Scientists have long been puzzled by how the creatures in this zone survive with so little light. Recent research suggests that many of these organisms have evolved ultra-black skin that absorbs over 99.5% of light, (B) making / made them virtually invisible to predators. This natural camouflage is far more effective than any man-made stealth technology. Studying these creatures could lead to the development of new light-absorbing materials. It is amazing (C) that / what secrets the deep ocean still holds.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

🔥 실전 모의고사 | 3회

  1. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 모두 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    The Japanese art of Kintsugi, or "golden joinery," is the practice of repairing broken pottery with lacquer mixed with powdered gold, silver, or platinum. The philosophy behind it is that the breakage and repair are part of the object's history, rather than something to disguise. This makes the repaired piece ⓐ even more beautiful than the original. This tradition is rooted in the Japanese philosophy of wabi-sabi, ⓑ which embraces the flawed or imperfect. Instead of seeing the cracks as a weakness, Kintsugi highlights ⓒ it as a strength, a testament to the object's resilience. It is a powerful metaphor for embracing our own imperfections and ⓓ see beauty in our scars.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

🔥 실전 모의고사 | 4회 (10문항)

  1. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    Nature seems to be constantly changing. If we observe nature closely, however, we discover (A) what / that there is constant tension between change and balance. In the case of human beings, the general shape and size of our body remains relatively constant while the cells within it are continually (B) replacing / being replaced. Likewise, the forest remains a forest, even while individual trees are removed by death and replaced by birth. The dead bodies of organisms in the forest are broken down and turned into soil, which in turn (C) nourishes / nourish other organisms. The elements of nature are continually changing, but nature itself remains constant.

  2. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    Amnesty International was formed in 1961 ① to defend human rights throughout the world. Created by a British lawyer, it now has more than 2.2 million members. The number of members working to help people who face unjust imprisonment ② is increasing. The main goal of Amnesty International is to assist those who ③ have been punished for their political beliefs. It also exposes human rights violations, encourages governments to change laws, and ④ conducts campaigns to educate the public about human rights. Amnesty International ⑤ awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 1977 for “having contributed to securing the ground for freedom, for justice, and thereby also for peace in the world.”

  3. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    Colton, a city in California, is currently ⓐ involved in a series of legal battles over how much ⓑ they should be prepared to pay to save an endangered fly: the Delhi Sands Flower-loving Fly. This tiny creature has the distinction of being the first fly which ⓒ was declared an endangered species in the U.S. Shortly after this fly ⓓ was listed as an endangered species, construction of a hospital parking lot was stopped. The hospital ⓔ has planned to construct its parking lot over seven acres of occupied fly habitat, but that suddenly became illegal. The hospital then had to spend $4 million redrawing its plans.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

🔥 실전 모의고사 | 4회

  1. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 모두 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    While humans ⓐ may have shifted from signs to speech long ago, Stokoe and Armstrong don't consider sign language primitive. In fact, they believe just the opposite. “My work in sign language,” says Stokoe, “is to let people know that the hearing-impaired are just as bright and able to handle abstract thought as ⓑ heard people, given equal education. Prejudice against them because they don't speak any languages ⓒ leading to all sorts of bad judgments about the hearing-impaired. ⓓ Recognizing the fact that we all probably owe a great deal to the era when language began in a gestural state might change some of that.”

  2. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    The phenomenon of biomimicry, (A) which / what involves learning from and mimicking strategies found in nature, has led to numerous innovations. For example, the design of the Japanese bullet train was inspired by the kingfisher's beak, (B) allowed / allowing it to enter tunnels much more quietly and efficiently. Similarly, Velcro was invented after a Swiss engineer examined the burrs that clung to his dog's fur. These examples show that nature, after billions of years of evolution, (C) offers / offer a wealth of brilliant solutions to human problems.

  3. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    The "wisdom of the crowd" refers to the idea that large groups of people are collectively smarter than individual experts. This principle is applied in various fields, from predicting market trends to ① improving search engine results. For the crowd's wisdom to emerge, however, certain conditions must be met. The group needs to be diverse, and each person's opinion must be independent of ② another. If people are influenced by the opinions of others, it can lead to herd behavior, ③ where the group makes poor decisions. A famous example is the stock market bubble, where irrational enthusiasm leads to inflated prices, ④ followed by a crash. This shows ⑤ what the crowd is not always wise.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

🔥 실전 모의고사 | 4회

  1. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    The human memory is not like a video camera, ⓐ recording events exactly as they happened. Instead, it is a reconstructive process that is susceptible to distortion. Psychologist Elizabeth Loftus's research has shown how easily memories can be altered. In one study, she had participants ⓑ watch a video of a car accident. When asked how fast the cars were going when they "smashed" into each other, participants gave a higher speed estimate than when the word "hit" ⓒ used. This phenomenon, known as the misinformation effect, has significant implications for the reliability of eyewitness testimony in the legal system, ⓓ which is often considered highly persuasive.

  2. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    The paradox of choice, ① described by psychologist Barry Schwartz, suggests that while we might believe that more choice is better, having too many options can actually lead to anxiety and dissatisfaction. When faced with an overwhelming number of choices, people often find ② it difficult to make a decision at all, a state known as analysis paralysis. And even after a choice is made, the abundance of alternatives makes ③ easy to imagine that a different choice might have been better. This can lead to regret and lower satisfaction with the chosen option. To combat this, it is suggested that we ④ limit our options and ⑤ learn to be content with a "good enough" choice.

  3. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    The placebo effect demonstrates the profound influence of the mind on the body. A placebo is a substance with no known medical effects, such as a sugar pill, (A) that / what is given to a patient who believes it to be a real medicine. Surprisingly, many patients report feeling better after taking a placebo. This effect is not just "in their heads"; research has shown that placebos can trigger the release of endorphins, the body's natural painkillers. This is why it is so important that new medical treatments (B) are / be tested in double-blind trials, where neither the patient nor the doctor knows who is receiving the real drug and who is receiving the placebo. This helps to ensure that the observed effects are due to the drug itself, not just the power of suggestion, (C) which is a powerful variable.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

🔥 실전 모의고사 | 4회

  1. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 모두 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    The development of agriculture was a pivotal moment in human history, ⓐ allowed societies to transition from nomadic lifestyles to settled communities. However, this shift also brought new challenges. One of the most significant ⓑ were the increased vulnerability to crop failure. A single drought or flood could devastate a community's food supply. Furthermore, the reliance on a few staple crops led to a less diverse diet, ⓒ which some historians believe contributed to a decline in overall health ⓓ compared to our hunter-gatherer ancestors.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

🔥 실전 모의고사 | 5회 (10문항)

  1. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    When we evolved from living on all fours to ① living on just two, life changed dramatically. We run marathons, walk shopping malls that are as large as entire counties, and ② spend summers hiking a trail that reaches from Georgia to Maine. No matter where we're walking, our feet endure some serious abuse. With twenty-six bones in our feet, we were made to move. But our feet don't have the natural shock absorbers other parts of the body ③ have. To better protect them, always ④ wearing well-cushioned walking or running shoes when you are on your feet for lengthy periods of time. Running shoes are usually a good option because they're well cushioned.

  2. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    Everybody knows that ① during the winter months, we often tend to feel “blue.” Some doctors say ② that this seasonal sad feeling is caused by a lack of sunlight. It is certainly true that people kept indoors all day suffer from “cabin fever,” a miserable feeling that you get when you are shut up inside for too long. But people who live in concrete buildings and walk paved streets all their lives ③ lack a lot more than sun. They may lose their link to fresh air, green scenery, and all the happiness ④ what nature has to offer. Of course, activities like gardening or hiking can help. But in the long run, we need to make spaces ⑤ where we can get the light and contact with nature that we were born for.

  3. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    Psychologist James Cutting claims that the originals of the most reproduced works of impressionism today ⓐ are thought to have been bought by five or six wealthy collectors in the late 19th century. The preferences of these men gave prestige to certain works, which made the works more likely to be hung in galleries and ⓑ print in anthologies. The popularity of these works grew over the years, and ⓒ they gained the interest of many people. ⓓ The more people liked them, the more they appeared in books and on posters. Meanwhile, academics and critics created sophisticated justifications for their excellence. After all, it's not just average citizens who tend to rate ⓔ that they see often more highly.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

🔥 실전 모의고사 | 5회

  1. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 모두 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    If people in a relationship spend time together, the relationship will ⓐ strengthen. Likewise, the enterprise will prosper if the partners agree on basic goals and plans. When common goals are discussed and worked toward, they provide a binding force that can compensate for many of the ⓑ predictably differences between people. For example, if a couple has the goal of saving money for a house, they will work toward ⓒ it, plan together, and arrive at specific steps to achieve that goal, thereby drawing themselves closer together. On the other hand, if one member wants to buy as many creature comforts as possible, while ⓓ another wants to save for a rainy day, the couple will be torn apart by these differences.

  2. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    Growth mindset, a concept developed by psychologist Carol Dweck, is the belief that abilities can be developed through dedication and hard work. This contrasts with a fixed mindset, the belief that abilities are innate gifts. Research shows that students with a growth mindset are more resilient in the face of challenges. They see failure not as a sign of unintelligence but as a springboard for growth. This makes them (A) to be / be more likely to persevere and ultimately succeed. It is therefore vital that educators and parents (B) foster / fosters a growth mindset in children, praising effort rather than innate talent. This helps children (C) develop / to developing a lifelong love of learning.

  3. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    The availability heuristic is a mental shortcut that relies on immediate examples that come to a person's mind when evaluating a specific topic. Because of this, we often misjudge the frequency of events. For example, after seeing several news reports about shark attacks, you might feel that the likelihood of ① being attacked by a shark is quite high. In reality, you are far more likely to be harmed by a falling coconut. The news media's focus on rare and dramatic events ② make them more available in our memory, ③ leading us to overestimate their probability. To make more accurate judgments, it is important ④ to consider statistical data rather than ⑤ relying solely on memorable anecdotes.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

🔥 실전 모의고사 | 5회

  1. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    The fight-or-flight response is a physiological reaction that occurs in response to a perceived harmful event. This response, ⓐ triggering by the release of hormones, prepares the body for intense physical activity. Your heart rate increases, your pupils dilate, and your awareness becomes hyper-focused. While this was essential for our ancestors' survival when facing predators, it is less adaptive in the modern world, ⓑ where most of our stressors are psychological. Chronic activation of this response can lead to health problems. This is ⓒ why learning stress-management techniques, such as deep breathing and meditation, ⓓ are so important for our long-term health.

  2. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

    The color of a food can have a profound impact on how we perceive its taste. In one experiment, participants were given cherry-flavored drinks that had been colored orange. Most of them reported that the drink tasted like orange. This shows ① how powerfully our visual expectations can override our other senses. Food companies are well aware of this, ② using artificial colors to make their products appear more appealing. This is why a strawberry yogurt is bright pink, even though the actual fruit content might be low. It is suggested that we ③ are more mindful of this manipulation and focus on the actual nutritional content rather than just the appearance, ④ which is often misleading. The more we understand these psychological tricks, the better choices we can make as consumers, just as our parents ⑤ did.

  3. (A), (B), (C)의 각 네모 안에서 어법에 맞는 표현으로 가장 적절한 것은?

    The "Ring of Fire" is a major area in the basin of the Pacific Ocean where a large number of earthquakes and volcanic eruptions occur. This horseshoe-shaped zone is home to 75% of the world's active volcanoes and is the site of about 90% of the world's earthquakes. This intense seismic activity is caused by the movement and collision of tectonic plates. The Pacific Plate, for example, is constantly pushing against the surrounding plates, (A) created / creating immense pressure. When this pressure is released, it results in an earthquake. It is crucial that people living in this region (B) build / builds earthquake-resistant structures and (C) are / be prepared for natural disasters.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

🔥 실전 모의고사 | 5회

  1. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 모두 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

    The invention of the printing press by Johannes Gutenberg in the 15th century was a turning point in history. It allowed information ⓐ to be disseminated far more quickly and widely than ever before. Before the printing press, books were rare and expensive, ⓑ copied by hand by scribes. This new technology made books ⓒ affordable for a much larger segment of the population, leading to a dramatic increase in literacy. The spread of ideas fueled the Renaissance, the Reformation, and the Scientific Revolution, forever ⓓ changed the structure of society.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 01 | TP 01 연습 문제

01
정답: collected

문장 해석

과학자들은 작년에 그들이 수집했던 데이터가 그들의 가설을 뒷받침한다고 믿는다.

핵심 해설

문장의 주 동사는 `believe`와 `supports`입니다. `the data` 뒤에는 목적격 관계대명사 `that`이 생략된 관계사절이 와야 합니다. 이 절 안에는 주어 `they`에 대한 동사가 필요하므로, 과거 시점(`last year`)에 맞는 동사 collected가 와야 합니다.

02
정답: conducted

문장 해석

그 팀에 의해 수행된 연구는 암 치료에 대한 유망한 결과를 보여준다.

핵심 해설

문장 전체의 동사는 `shows`입니다. 빈칸은 주어인 `The research`를 수식하는 자리입니다. 연구는 스스로 수행하는 것이 아니라 '수행되는' 것이므로, 수동의 의미를 가진 과거분사 conducted가 와야 합니다.

03
정답: Taking

문장 해석

규칙적인 운동을 하는 것은 신체적, 정신적 건강을 모두 유지하는 데 도움이 된다.

핵심 해설

문장의 동사는 `helps`입니다. 이 동사에 대한 주어 역할을 할 수 있는 것은 명사 형태입니다. '운동을 하는 것'이라는 의미의 주어가 필요하므로 동명사 Taking이 와야 합니다. 명령문이 아니므로 동사원형 `Take`는 올 수 없습니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 01 | TP 01 연습 문제

04
정답: allowing

문장 해석

정부는 새로운 정책들을 발표했고, 그리하여 더 많은 학생들이 재정 지원을 받을 수 있게 되었다.

핵심 해설

`thus`는 접속사가 아닌 부사입니다. 따라서 절과 절을 연결할 수 없습니다. 앞 문장에 대한 부가적인 결과를 나타내는 분사구문이 필요하며, '더 많은 학생들이 지원받도록 허용하면서'라는 능동의 의미이므로 현재분사 allowing이 와야 합니다.

05
정답: have

문장 해석

이전에는 결코 이렇게 많은 사람들이 소셜 미디어 플랫폼을 통해 연결된 적이 없었다.

핵심 해설

부정 부사구 `Never before`가 문장 맨 앞에 와서 주어와 동사가 도치된 구문입니다. 이 문장의 진짜 주어는 동사 뒤에 있는 `so many people`(복수)이므로, 복수 동사인 have가 와야 합니다.

PART I | UNIT 01 | TP 02 연습 문제

01
정답: promote

문장 해석

그 회의는 연구자들이 함께 모여, 그들의 연구 결과를 공유하고, 협력을 증진하는 것을 목표로 한다.

핵심 해설

`to bring together`, `to share`, `(to) promote`가 병렬 구조를 이루고 있습니다. 따라서 동사원형 promote가 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 01 | TP 02 연습 문제

02
정답: for her

문장 해석

그녀는 학문적 성취뿐만 아니라 지역 사회 봉사로도 알려져 있다.

핵심 해설

`not only A but also B` 구문에서 A와 B는 문법적으로 동일한 형태여야 합니다. A가 `for her academic achievements`라는 전치사구이므로, B도 동일한 형태인 for her community service가 되어야 합니다.

03
정답: affect

문장 해석

새로운 정책은 학생과 교사 모두에게 상당한 영향을 미칠 것이다.

핵심 해설

조동사 `will` 뒤에는 동사원형이 와야 합니다. `affect`는 '영향을 미치다'라는 동사이고, `effect`는 '효과, 영향'이라는 명사입니다. 따라서 동사 affect가 정답입니다.

04
정답: or

문장 해석

오늘 보고서를 제출하든지, 아니면 내일 불이익을 받게 될 것이다.

핵심 해설

`Either A or B`는 'A이거나 B이거나'라는 의미의 상관접속사 구문입니다. 따라서 or가 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 01 | TP 02 연습 문제

05
정답: restructure, hire

문장 해석

관리자는 부서를 재구성하고 새로운 직원을 고용하기로 결정했다.

핵심 해설

동사 `decided`의 목적어로 `to restructure`와 `(to) hire`가 병렬 구조로 연결되어 있습니다. 따라서 동사원형 restructure와 hire가 정답입니다.

06
정답: both, and

문장 해석

그 예술가의 작품은 혁신적이면서 동시에 상업적으로도 성공적이다.

핵심 해설

`both A and B`는 'A와 B 둘 다'라는 의미의 상관접속사 구문입니다. 따라서 both와 and가 정답입니다.

07
정답: work

문장 해석

성공을 이루기 위해서는 헌신, 인내, 그리고 현명한 노력이 필요하다.

핵심 해설

동사 `requires`의 목적어로 `dedication`, `perseverance`, `work`가 병렬 구조를 이루고 있습니다. 모두 명사 형태여야 하므로 work가 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 01 | TP 02 연습 문제

08
정답: with

문장 해석

그 연구는 규칙적으로 운동하는 참가자들과 주로 앉아서 생활하는 사람들을 비교했다.

핵심 해설

`compare A with B`는 'A와 B를 비교하다'라는 의미의 구문입니다. 따라서 with가 정답입니다.

09
정답: but also

문장 해석

그 회사는 제품을 개선했을 뿐만 아니라 고객 서비스도 향상시켰다.

핵심 해설

`not only A but also B`는 'A뿐만 아니라 B도'라는 의미의 상관접속사 구문입니다. 따라서 but also가 정답입니다.

10
정답: take

문장 해석

상황에 대해 불평하기보다는, 그녀는 조치를 취하기로 결정했다.

핵심 해설

동사 `decided`는 목적어로 to부정사를 취합니다. 따라서 to take가 와야 하므로 take가 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 03 연습 문제

01
정답: prescribed

문장 해석

의사가 처방한 그 약은 매우 효과적인 것으로 증명되었다.

핵심 해설

의미상 주어인 `The medicine`은 '처방되는' 것이므로 수동 관계입니다. 따라서 과거분사 prescribed가 정답입니다.

02
정답: Known

문장 해석

관대함으로 알려져 있었기 때문에, 그는 수백만 달러를 자선단체에 기부했다.

핵심 해설

분사구문의 의미상 주어는 주절의 주어인 `he`입니다. 그는 '알려진' 것이므로 수동 관계입니다. 따라서 과거분사 Known이 정답입니다.

03
정답: equipped

문장 해석

최신 기술을 갖춘 그 회의실은 회의를 주최할 것이다.

핵심 해설

의미상 주어인 `The conference room`은 기술을 '갖추게 된' 것이므로 수동 관계입니다. 따라서 과거분사 equipped가 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 03 연습 문제

04
정답: fascinating

문장 해석

그녀는 그 강의가 매혹적이라고 생각했고 상세한 필기를 했다.

핵심 해설

5형식 동사 `found`의 목적격 보어 자리입니다. 의미상 주어인 목적어 `the lecture`가 감정을 '유발하는' 주체이므로 현재분사 fascinating이 정답입니다.

05
정답: Faced

문장 해석

이 도전에 직면하여, 그 팀은 혁신적인 해결책을 개발했다.

핵심 해설

분사구문의 의미상 주어는 주절의 주어인 `the team`입니다. 팀이 도전에 '직면하게 된' 것이므로 수동 관계입니다. 따라서 과거분사 Faced가 정답입니다.

06
정답: collected

문장 해석

5년 이상에 걸쳐 수집된 데이터는 우리의 가설을 뒷받침한다.

핵심 해설

의미상 주어인 `The data`는 '수집되는' 것이므로 수동 관계입니다. 따라서 과거분사 collected가 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 03 연습 문제

07
정답: concerned

문장 해석

오염에 대해 우려하는 시민들이 항의하기 위해 모였다.

핵심 해설

의미상 주어인 `Citizens`가 오염에 대해 '걱정하는 감정을 느끼는' 객체이므로 과거분사 concerned가 정답입니다.

08
정답: constructed

문장 해석

작년에 건설된 그 건물은 건축상을 받았다.

핵심 해설

의미상 주어인 `The building`은 '건설되는' 것이므로 수동 관계입니다. 따라서 과거분사 constructed가 정답입니다.

09
정답: Exhausted

문장 해석

긴 여정으로 지쳐서, 여행객들은 호텔에서 쉬었다.

핵심 해설

분사구문의 의미상 주어는 주절의 주어인 `travelers`입니다. 여행객들이 '지친 감정을 느끼는' 객체이므로 과거분사 Exhausted가 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 03 연습 문제

10
정답: obtained

문장 해석

실험에서 얻어진 결과는 놀라웠다.

핵심 해설

의미상 주어인 `The results`는 '얻어지는' 것이므로 수동 관계입니다. 따라서 과거분사 obtained가 정답입니다.

11
정답: Given

문장 해석

상황을 고려할 때, 우리는 연기할 수밖에 없었다.

핵심 해설

`Given`은 '~을 고려하면'이라는 의미의 전치사처럼 쓰이는 분사구문입니다. 관용적으로 Given을 사용합니다.

12
정답: training

문장 해석

수년간 훈련한 선수들은 마침내 그들의 목표를 달성했다.

핵심 해설

의미상 주어인 `The athletes`가 '훈련을 하는' 주체이므로 능동 관계입니다. 따라서 현재분사 training이 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 03 연습 문제

13
정답: Located

문장 해석

도심에 위치해 있어서, 그 박물관은 많은 방문객을 끌어들인다.

핵심 해설

분사구문의 의미상 주어는 주절의 주어인 `the museum`입니다. 박물관은 '위치해 있는' 것이므로 수동 관계입니다. 따라서 과거분사 Located가 정답입니다.

14
정답: encouraging

문장 해석

교수는 학생들의 반응이 고무적이라고 생각했다.

핵심 해설

5형식 동사 `found`의 목적격 보어 자리입니다. 의미상 주어인 목적어 `the students' responses`가 감정을 '유발하는' 주체이므로 현재분사 encouraging이 정답입니다.

15
정답: Absorbed

문장 해석

일에 몰두해 있어서, 그는 시간이 가는 줄 몰랐다.

핵심 해설

분사구문의 의미상 주어는 주절의 주어인 `he`입니다. 그가 일에 '몰두하게 된' 것이므로 수동 관계입니다. 따라서 과거분사 Absorbed가 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 04 연습 문제

01
정답: Understanding

문장 해석

문화적 차이를 이해하는 것은 인내심을 요구한다.

핵심 해설

동사 `requires`의 주어 자리입니다. 뒤에 목적어 `the cultural differences`를 바로 취할 수 있는 것은 동명사 Understanding입니다. 명사 `Understanding of`도 가능하지만, 동명사가 더 간결하고 직접적입니다.

02
정답: development

문장 해석

신기술의 발전은 급속도로 가속화되었다.

핵심 해설

동사 `has accelerated`의 주어 자리입니다. 뒤에 `of new technologies`라는 전치사구가 오므로, 목적어를 직접 취할 필요가 없는 명사 development가 정답입니다.

03
정답: being given

문장 해석

학생들은 명확한 지시를 받는 것을 고맙게 생각한다.

핵심 해설

동사 `appreciate`는 동명사를 목적어로 취합니다. 학생들이 지시를 '받는' 것이므로 수동의 의미를 가진 동명사 being given이 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 04 연습 문제

04
정답: to have been completed

문장 해석

그 프로젝트는 예정보다 앞서 완료된 것으로 보인다.

핵심 해설

프로젝트가 완료된 것은 '보이는' 시점(`appears`)보다 더 이전의 일이므로, 완료 부정사인 to have been completed가 정답입니다. 또한 프로젝트는 '완료되는' 것이므로 수동태 형태여야 합니다.

05
정답: To successfully implement

문장 해석

그 계획을 성공적으로 실행하는 것은 협력을 필요로 한다.

핵심 해설

동사 `requires`의 주어 자리입니다. `successfully`는 부사로, 준동사인 `implement`를 수식할 수 있습니다. 문장의 주어 역할을 하는 to부정사구 To successfully implement가 정답입니다. 동명사구 `Successfully implementing`도 문법적으로 가능합니다.

06
정답: not studying

문장 해석

나는 시험공부를 더 열심히 하지 않은 것을 후회한다.

핵심 해설

동사 `regret`은 과거에 한 일에 대한 후회를 나타낼 때 동명사를 목적어로 취합니다. 준동사의 부정은 바로 앞에 `not`을 붙이므로 not studying이 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 04 연습 문제

07
정답: to be reviewed

문장 해석

그 서류들은 제출 전에 검토될 필요가 있다.

핵심 해설

동사 `need`는 to부정사를 목적어로 취할 수 있습니다. 서류는 '검토되는' 것이므로 수동의 의미를 가진 to be reviewed가 정답입니다. `need`는 동명사를 목적어로 취할 때 수동의 의미를 가지므로 `reviewing`도 가능합니다.

08
정답: Having carefully considered

문장 해석

모든 선택지를 신중하게 고려한 후에, 그들은 결정을 내렸다.

핵심 해설

분사구문에서, 주절의 동작(`made a decision`)보다 먼저 일어난 일을 나타낼 때는 완료 분사구문 `Having p.p.`를 사용합니다. 따라서 Having carefully considered가 정답입니다.

09
정답: to have met

문장 해석

그녀는 작년에 그 유명한 작가를 만났다고 주장한다.

핵심 해설

작가를 만난 것(`last year`)은 주장하는 시점(`claims`)보다 더 이전의 일이므로, 완료 부정사 to have met이 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 04 연습 문제

10
정답: analysis

문장 해석

시장 동향 분석은 긍정적인 성장을 보여준다.

핵심 해설

동사 `shows`의 주어 자리입니다. 뒤에 `of market trends`라는 전치사구가 오므로, 목적어를 직접 취할 필요가 없는 명사 analysis가 정답입니다.

11
정답: Being constantly criticized

문장 해석

끊임없이 비판받는 것은 그의 자신감에 영향을 미쳤다.

핵심 해설

동사 `affected`의 주어 자리입니다. '비판받는 것'이라는 수동의 의미를 가진 동명사 주어 Being constantly criticized가 정답입니다.

12
정답: making

문장 해석

그는 인터뷰 동안 눈을 마주치는 것을 피했다.

핵심 해설

동사 `avoided`는 동명사를 목적어로 취합니다. 따라서 making이 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 05 연습 문제

01
정답: to lock

문장 해석

나갈 때 문 잠그는 것을 기억해라.

핵심 해설

`remember` 뒤에 to부정사가 오면 '미래에 ~할 것을 기억하다'라는 의미입니다. 앞으로 할 일이므로 to lock이 정답입니다.

02
정답: meeting

문장 해석

나는 그 유명한 과학자를 만났던 것을 결코 잊지 못할 것이다.

핵심 해설

`forget` 뒤에 동명사가 오면 '과거에 ~했던 것을 잊다'라는 의미입니다. 과거의 경험이므로 meeting이 정답입니다.

03
정답: to open

문장 해석

그녀는 창문을 열려고 노력했지만, 꼼짝도 하지 않았다.

핵심 해설

`try` 뒤에 to부정사가 오면 '~하려고 노력하다, 애쓰다'라는 의미입니다. 문맥상 노력의 의미가 강하므로 to open이 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 05 연습 문제

04
정답: using

문장 해석

그는 다른 접근법을 시험 삼아 사용해 보았고, 그것은 효과가 있었다.

핵심 해설

`try` 뒤에 동명사가 오면 '시험 삼아 ~해보다'라는 의미입니다. 문맥상 실험적인 시도를 의미하므로 using이 정답입니다.

05
정답: to inform

문장 해석

당신의 지원서가 거절되었음을 알리게 되어 유감입니다.

핵심 해설

`regret` 뒤에 to부정사가 오면 '미래에 ~하게 되어 유감이다'라는 의미로, 주로 안 좋은 소식을 전할 때 사용됩니다. 따라서 to inform이 정답입니다.

06
정답: leaving

문장 해석

이전 직장을 그만둔 것을 후회하나요?

핵심 해설

`regret` 뒤에 동명사가 오면 '과거에 ~했던 것을 후회하다'라는 의미입니다. 과거의 행동에 대한 후회이므로 leaving이 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 05 연습 문제

07
정답: working

문장 해석

기계가 갑자기 작동을 멈췄다.

핵심 해설

`stop` 뒤에 동명사가 오면 '~하는 것을 멈추다'라는 의미입니다. 기계가 작동하던 것을 멈춘 것이므로 working이 정답입니다.

08
정답: to have

문장 해석

우리는 길가 식당에서 점심을 먹기 위해 멈췄다.

핵심 해설

`stop` 뒤에 to부정사가 오면 '~하기 위해 멈추다'라는 목적의 의미입니다. 점심을 먹을 목적으로 멈춘 것이므로 to have가 정답입니다.

09
정답: to say

문장 해석

그 발언으로 무엇을 말하려는 의도인가요?

핵심 해설

`mean` 뒤에 to부정사가 오면 '~할 의도이다'라는 의미입니다. 따라서 to say가 정답입니다. (`mean v-ing`는 '~을 의미하다'입니다.)

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 05 연습 문제

10
정답: contacting

문장 해석

일정을 변경하는 것은 모든 참가자에게 연락하는 것을 의미한다.

핵심 해설

`mean` 뒤에 동명사가 오면 '~을 의미하다'라는 뜻입니다. 따라서 contacting이 정답입니다.

11
정답: to answer

문장 해석

연설을 마친 후, 그는 계속해서 질문에 답했다.

핵심 해설

`go on` 뒤에 to부정사가 오면 '(하던 것을 멈추고) 이어서 다른 것을 하다'라는 의미입니다. 연설을 끝내고 질문에 답하는 새로운 행동을 시작했으므로 to answer가 정답입니다.

12
정답: performing

문장 해석

비에도 불구하고, 콘서트는 예정대로 계속 진행되었다.

핵심 해설

`go on` 뒤에 동명사가 오면 '계속해서 ~하다'라는 의미입니다. 콘서트가 계속된 것이므로 performing이 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 05 연습 문제

13
정답: to tell/telling

문장 해석

그 노인은 젊은 시절에 대한 이야기를 하기 시작했다.

핵심 해설

동사 `begin`은 to부정사와 동명사 둘 다 목적어로 취할 수 있으며 의미 차이가 거의 없습니다. 따라서 to tell과 telling 모두 정답입니다.

14
정답: to rain/raining

문장 해석

정오 무렵에 비가 세차게 내리기 시작했다.

핵심 해설

동사 `start`는 to부정사와 동명사 둘 다 목적어로 취할 수 있으며 의미 차이가 거의 없습니다. 따라서 to rain과 raining 모두 정답입니다.

15
정답: cooking

문장 해석

그녀는 주말에 가족을 위해 요리하는 것을 아주 좋아한다.

핵심 해설

동사 `love`는 to부정사와 동명사 둘 다 목적어로 취할 수 있으며 의미 차이가 거의 없습니다. 따라서 cooking이 정답입니다. (to cook도 가능)

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 05 연습 문제

16
정답: to disturb

문장 해석

당신을 방해하는 것이 싫지만, 이건 급한 일입니다.

핵심 해설

동사 `hate`는 to부정사와 동명사 둘 다 목적어로 취할 수 있으며 의미 차이가 거의 없습니다. 따라서 to disturb가 정답입니다. (disturbing도 가능)

17
정답: revising

문장 해석

그 보고서는 제출 전에 수정될 필요가 있다.

핵심 해설

동사 `need` 뒤에 동명사가 오면 수동의 의미를 가집니다 (`need revising` = `need to be revised`). 따라서 revising이 정답입니다.

18
정답: handling

문장 해석

이 상황은 극도의 주의를 기울여 다룰 필요가 있다.

핵심 해설

동사 `require` 뒤에 동명사가 오면 수동의 의미를 가집니다 (`require handling` = `require to be handled`). 따라서 handling이 정답입니다.

Vera's Flavor 편입영어 구문독해 Bridge 편

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 05 연습 문제

19
정답: considering

문장 해석

그 제안은 진지하게 고려할 가치가 있다.

핵심 해설

관용 표현 `be worth v-ing`는 '~할 가치가 있다'는 의미입니다. 따라서 동명사 considering이 정답입니다.

20
정답: wondering

문장 해석

나는 그 결과에 대해 궁금해하지 않을 수 없다.

핵심 해설

관용 표현 `can't help v-ing`는 '~하지 않을 수 없다'는 의미입니다. 따라서 동명사 wondering이 정답입니다.

PART I | UNIT 02 | TP 07 연습 문제

01
정답: living
